bugs.description: Score text shows '0' briefly on game start even when HUD init uses GameState.score - this is correct behavior since score resets, but the '0' flickers before first car passes.
Malfunction warning bar at bottom overlaps with Stage text slightly on smaller viewports. The MALFUNCTION! text and bar could use better vertical spacing.
During rapid light toggling, the yellow transition spark particles sometimes appear at slightly offset positions when toggles happen faster than debounce window.
Crash debris (pizza, rubber duck, etc.) confirmed spawning correctly during collisions, but they appear very small (16x16) and may be hard to appreciate on mobile screens.

passed_checks: Game loads without console errors
Canvas renders correctly (390x680)
Title screen: PLAY button visible and functional
Help button (?) works and shows How To Play screen
4 traffic light buttons visible at N/S/E/W positions
Lights toggle between red/green with yellow transition animation
Cars spawn from all 4 edges and drive toward intersection
Cars stop at red lights correctly
Cars flow through on green lights
Perpendicular green lights cause crashes when cars collide
Crash effects: explosion circle, debris (duck/pizza/toilet/banana/coffee), smoke, screen flash, camera shake
Inactivity death at ~8s triggers MALFUNCTION warning bar then chain crash
Game Over screen shows score, stats, NEW BEST, Play Again and Menu buttons
Play Again restarts game correctly with score=0, lives=3
Menu button returns to title screen correctly
Stage progression works (reached Stage 2)
Combo system visible in HUD (x17 combo observed with 2.5x multiplier)
Near-miss sparks and +25 bonus points confirmed
Life icons update correctly when crashes happen
Score count-up animation on Game Over screen
Rapid tap stress test: no crashes, no console errors
High score persists across sessions via localStorage
Decorative cars animate on menu screen
Sound toggle button visible on menu

instant_understand.note: Tap lights to toggle green/red - immediately intuitive

one_more_round.note: After MALFUNCTION death, immediately wanted to try again with better strategy

satisfying_feedback.note: Light toggle has yellow transition + spark particles + vibration. Crashes have explosion + debris + screen shake + flash. Combo multiplier text pulses.

clear_fail_state.note: 3 lives shown as car icons, each crash visibly removes one. MALFUNCTION bar provides countdown urgency.

escalating_challenge.note: Stage 1 has only sedans at slow speed. Stage 2+ increases car speed and spawn rate. Vehicle mix diversifies.

score_meaningful.note: Combo multiplier (up to 5x) rewards sustained traffic flow. Near-miss bonus rewards risky timing. Stage clear bonuses.

death_under_30s_idle.note: Inactivity death at 8 seconds is aggressive and effective. MALFUNCTION warning at 6s gives urgency.

restart_under_2s.note: Play Again button immediately starts new game, very fast restart loop.

no_confusion_moments.note: Light positions match directions clearly. HUD is clean. Only minor issue: might not immediately understand which light combos are safe vs dangerous without reading help.

want_to_beat_score.note: NEW BEST! animation with confetti is motivating. Score displayed on menu. Combo system creates skill ceiling.

qualitative_notes: The core gameplay loop of managing perpendicular traffic flows is genuinely engaging - it creates a real-time puzzle that requires spatial awareness and timing.
The combo system (x5 at stage 1, x17 at stage 2) gives satisfying score feedback for sustained good play.
Near-miss mechanic (+25 CLOSE! bonus) adds a fun risk-reward element.
Crash effects are entertaining - rubber ducks, pizza slices, and toilets flying from car crashes is funny and fits the chaotic theme.
The MALFUNCTION mechanic with the countdown bar creates genuine tension - you cant just set all lights to red and relax.
Emergency double-tap center stop is a smart panic button mechanic.
Stage progression with Rush Hour events adds variety.
Weakness: Early stages are a bit slow since only sedans spawn and traffic is sparse.
Weakness: All deaths during testing were from MALFUNCTION (inactivity) rather than crashes, suggesting the crash collision detection is a bit generous - perpendicular cars can sometimes slip through.

sessions.notes: First session, learning controls. Died to inactivity while testing perpendicular lights.
Strategic alternating N+S / E+W flow. Reached stage 2, built impressive combo. Score improved 7x from session 1.
Attempted riskier perpendicular timing. Lower score but still engaging.

replay_observations: High score correctly persists (615 shown as Best on game over in session 3).
Only sedans observed in stages 1-2. Trucks appear at stage 4+, sports at 7+, ambulances at 11+. Would take longer play to see vehicle variety.
Difficulty scales via spawn interval decrease and car speed increase.
Score improvement across sessions shows skill ceiling exists.
Core strategy (alternate parallel pairs) becomes repetitive - could use more variety in early stages.
No daily challenges, achievements, or progression systems beyond high score.
The game lacks a sense of progression hooks - no unlockables, no cosmetics, no milestones.
Rush Hour events at stage 10, 20 etc provide variety but most players wont reach them quickly.

verdict_reasoning: Traffic Light Conductor is a well-executed traffic management game with solid core mechanics, good juice (crash debris, combo multipliers, camera shake), and effective difficulty scaling. Zero console errors, no blocker bugs. The game is immediately understandable and creates genuine tension through the MALFUNCTION timer and perpendicular traffic conflicts. Weaknesses are limited to early-stage pacing (only sedans until stage 4) and lack of meta-progression hooks for long-term replay. The 79.0 composite score exceeds the 70-point SHIP threshold.
